task_completion.description: Agent opens a web browser, navigates to the target sites, and opens the Developer Tools Network tab.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a web browser open to one or more of the target sites (e.g., Hacker News, Wikipedia) with the Developer Tools pane open and the 'Network' tab selected, displaying network requests.

task_completion.description: Agent exports network data as HAR files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent interacting with the 'Export HAR' button in the browser DevTools, or a file save dialog saving a file with a '.har' extension.

task_completion.description: At least 3 HAR files are saved in the required directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the contents of '~/Documents/har_exports/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/har_exports/') with at least 3 distinct files ending in '.har'.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the performance summary report JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, IDE, or terminal showing the creation or editing of a file named 'performance_report.json' in the '~/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: The performance report contains the required structure and covers all 5 target sites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'performance_report.json' are visible, showing top-level keys for all 5 sites (news.ycombinator.com, en.wikipedia.org, developer.mozilla.org, www.python.org, docs.github.com). Each site's entry must contain the keys: 'total_requests', 'total_transfer_size_kb', 'js_requests', 'css_requests', 'image_requests', and 'largest_resource_url'.

integrity.description: Agent actually captured network traffic using a browser or valid automation tool, rather than faking the HAR files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser DevTools Network tab is visible showing a populated waterfall of actual network requests, or a script (e.g., Playwright/Puppeteer) is shown actively navigating and capturing HAR data.

integrity.description: The JSON report contains realistic data, not mocked or zeroed-out templates.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in 'performance_report.json' show realistic, non-zero integers for request counts and floats for transfer sizes, indicating actual analysis was performed.

integrity.description: The exported HAR files are genuine and not empty placeholder files.
integrity.visual_evidence: File sizes for the .har files in the file manager or terminal (e.g., via 'ls -l') are greater than zero, typically in the kilobytes or megabytes range.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target sites to be audited: news.ycombinator.com, en.wikipedia.org, developer.mozilla.org, www.python.org, and docs.github.com. The agent must export at least 3 HAR files to '/home/ga/Documents/har_exports/'. The final report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/performance_report.json' and must include all 5 sites with the exact keys: total_requests, total_transfer_size_kb, js_requests, css_requests, image_requests, and largest_resource_url.
